title: toona inodora
intro: Toona inodora, also known as Meranti, is a deciduous tree native to Southeast Asia, characterized by its distinctive, aromatic wood and medicinal properties. The plant contains various bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and terpenoids, which exhibit anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it a valuable source for traditional medicine. The health benefits of Toona inodora include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and exhibit hepatoprotective effects, among others. In traditional herbal preparations, the wood and bark of Toona inodora are often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are consumed to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
